worked_derivation:
Brouncker (1,0,0,2,1):  a(n)=n^2,  b(n)=2n+1.
BT char poly  lam^2 - 2 lam - 1 = 0  has roots 1 +- sqrt(2),
ratio = (1+sqrt2)/(1-sqrt2) = -(3 + 2 sqrt 2).
D = 2 b_top^2 = 8 -> Class B locus, fingerprints to PIII(D6).
Theta_inf = log(3+2 sqrt 2) / (2 pi) (irrational).
Limit L = 4/pi (Brouncker), confirms PIII tau-fn at the
well-known degeneration parameters (alpha,beta,gamma,delta)
= (1, -1, 1, -1)/8 -- canonical 'Brouncker frame' for PIII(D6).
q-expansion of Brouncker convergents (P_n/Q_n) gives the
Wallis product, identical to the Pochhammer ratio in the
tau function at half-integer parameters.

Class A example (-8, 0, 0, 6, -1): a2/b1^2 = -8/36 = -2/9.
BT char poly  lam^2 - b1 lam - a2 = 0; with b1=3k, a2=-2k^2 the
roots are lam = k, lam = 2k. Ratio = 2 (k=2 integer resonance).
Theta_inf = 1/2 (Schwarz-list / Picard candidate).
Theta_0 = a0 / b0^2 (rational); n_star = -b0/b1 (rational).
All four monodromy proxies lie in Q with denominators in
{1,2,3,9}, fingerprinting into the PVI-rigid-Q stratum and
(when denominators reduce to {1,2}) the Picard sub-locus.
q-expansion of P_n/Q_n at large n exhibits the n^{1/2} log n
Stokes correction (Class A signature), consistent with PVI
tau-function behaviour near the algebraic Picard 4-orbit.
